source.title: MM-927: Moon Spec Doc Architecture Alignment
source.text:
MM-927: Moon Spec Doc Architecture Alignment

Current state
MoonMind is in a good position for this pivot. The implemented work establishes the core doctrine: MoonSpec now has a Documentation Architecture Standard that is explicitly a MoonSpec-level strategy, not a repo-specific migration plan, and it extends rather than replaces the MoonSpec Document Model. The standard defines the vocabulary, five canonical declarative viewpoints, module-owned contracts, same-class precedence, and embedded rationale policy.
The base model is also wired correctly: canonical docs under docs/ describe desired state, temporary execution artifacts under specs//artifacts/ are disposable derived views, and imperative working docs live under docs/tmp/ or gitignored handoff paths. The read order now explicitly places the Document Model first, then the Documentation Architecture Standard, then project-local architecture/design/contract docs, then optional implementation scratch.
The current MoonSpec workflow has already moved beyond vanilla Spec Kit in an important way. moonspec-breakdown classifies input as canonical declarative, declarative text, or imperative override; fails fast on imperative checklists without explicit override; and treats breakdown output as a temporary derived view whose source document remains authoritative. It extracts DESIGN-REQ-* coverage points, maps them to independently testable stories, and writes the result under gitignored artifacts/story-breakdowns/ rather than creating durable specs.
The rest of the skill chain has also been adjusted around docs as higher authority: moonspec-specify records the canonical source document and treats spec.md as a temporary execution artifact, moonspec-implement writes a discovery ledger for doc drift, moonspec-verify reports Source Document Drift, and moonspec-doc-reconcile runs after FULLY_IMPLEMENTED to update canonical docs only when verified evidence definitely requires it.
The orchestration layer is aligned with that model. moonspec-orchestrate now includes moonspec-doc-reconcile as the final stage, and the Jira orchestrate preset runs doc reconciliation before PR creation, writes a structured reconciliation artifact, and requires doc edits from reconciliation to be committed in the same PR as the implementation.
So the main assessment is:
MoonSpec already treats canonical docs as the higher authority, but the execution path is still Spec-Kit-shaped: docs feed breakdown/spec/plan/tasks, and those temporary artifacts drive implementation. The next evolution is to make canonical docs the first-class object MoonSpec generates, indexes, slices, verifies, and reconciles.
Immediate cleanup before the next evolution
I would do a small stabilization pass before adding bigger capabilities.
First, fix a naming inconsistency in the standard. Section 3.2 says a Module Architecture View prefers Architecture.md or Overview.md inside the module directory, but the later naming section says <ModuleName>ModuleArchitecture.md is the preferred filename. Since you explicitly chose <ModuleName>ModuleArchitecture.md, update Section 3.2 and downstream examples to match.
Second, clarify System / Feature Design View. The standard currently frames these as proposed or in-progress designs that must be promoted into architecture views once implemented. That is good for feature proposals, but your stated goal includes durable “system documents” inside module doc sets. I would split the concept mentally, even if not as two formal viewpoints yet:
<SystemName>System.md
Durable module-scoped desired-state system document.

<FeatureName>Design.md
Transitional or proposal-oriented design document that may be superseded
after implementation.
Third, align the templates with the metadata standard. The standard’s canonical header expects fields such as Authority, Owning Surface, Related Docs, and Related Implementation, but the current templates use a lighter header with Owner, Last Updated, and Related. The templates should be the golden path; make them match the standard exactly.
Fourth, close or update stale temporary plans. The migration plan records unresolved conflicts C1-C4, while another temp alignment record says there are no unresolved documentation-authority conflicts. Since both are imperative working docs, reconcile the status and delete/archive stale planning material once it has served its purpose.
Recommended MoonSpec evolution model
The next architecture should be:
Canonical docs
-> doc index / authority graph
-> doc slice / story candidate
-> temporary implementation packet
-> implementation and verification
-> canonical doc reconciliation
-> PR closes with docs and code aligned
The important inversion is that spec.md should stop being the conceptual center. It can remain as a temporary adapter for compatibility, but MoonSpec’s native domain should become:
Architecture Description
Viewpoint
View
Module Doc Set
Module Contract Specification
Doc claim / invariant / requirement
Doc slice
Implementation packet
Reconciliation result
That maps directly onto the standard’s vocabulary: a project’s docs tree is the Architecture Description; views conform to viewpoints; module doc sets group module-owned documents; and contracts live with their owning module.
Phase 1 — Make canonical docs addressable
The first major capability should be a MoonSpec doc index.
Today, breakdown generates sequential DESIGN-REQ-* IDs per run. That is useful, but those IDs are temporary: the same doc processed tomorrow can produce different IDs or slightly different coverage boundaries. To make docs the source of truth, the docs themselves need stable addressable claims.
Add a lightweight convention for canonical docs:
### DOC-REQ-001 — Workflow runs preserve source document traceability
### CONTRACT-001 — Agent runtime step payload
### INV-001 — Workflows never perform side effects directly
### NON-GOAL-001 — No global contracts directory
Then add a moonspec-doc-index tool or skill that emits a machine-readable index.

designSummary: MM-927 describes a MoonSpec evolution from Spec-Kit-shaped temporary execution artifacts toward canonical documentation as the first-class object that is generated, indexed, sliced, verified, and reconciled. The selected Jira brief is declarative text rather than a file-backed canonical document, so no canonical claim IDs are fabricated. The breakdown keeps immediate documentation-standard stabilization separate from the larger doc-index, doc-slice, workflow, and reconciliation capabilities so each story can be specified and tested independently.
coverageGate: PASS - every major design point is owned by at least one story.

coveragePoints.title: Canonical docs remain higher authority
coveragePoints.explanation: MoonSpec must treat docs/ canonical documents as desired-state authority while temporary artifacts remain disposable derived views.

coveragePoints.title: Document classes and read order stay explicit
coveragePoints.explanation: The Document Model, Documentation Architecture Standard, project docs, and optional scratch material must be ordered and classified consistently.

coveragePoints.title: Breakdown rejects unconfirmed imperative input
coveragePoints.explanation: moonspec-breakdown must classify input, fail fast on imperative checklists without override, and write derived stories under artifacts/story-breakdowns.

coveragePoints.title: Downstream skills preserve source traceability
coveragePoints.explanation: specify, implement, verify, and doc-reconcile must keep canonical source references, discovery ledgers, source drift reporting, and verified reconciliation behavior.

coveragePoints.title: Orchestration reconciles docs before PR completion
coveragePoints.explanation: moonspec-orchestrate and the Jira preset must run doc reconciliation before PR creation and commit required doc edits with implementation work.

coveragePoints.title: MoonSpec domain becomes doc-native
coveragePoints.explanation: MoonSpec’s native concepts should include architecture descriptions, viewpoints, views, module doc sets, contracts, claims, slices, implementation packets, and reconciliation results.

coveragePoints.title: Stabilize module architecture naming
coveragePoints.source_section: Immediate cleanup before the next evolution
coveragePoints.explanation: The documentation standard must consistently prefer <ModuleName>ModuleArchitecture.md where that convention has been chosen.

coveragePoints.title: Clarify system documents versus feature designs
coveragePoints.source_section: Immediate cleanup before the next evolution
coveragePoints.explanation: Durable <SystemName>System.md documents should be distinguished from transitional <FeatureName>Design.md proposal documents.

coveragePoints.title: Templates match canonical metadata headers
coveragePoints.source_section: Immediate cleanup before the next evolution
coveragePoints.explanation: Documentation templates must use the standard header fields such as Authority, Owning Surface, Related Docs, and Related Implementation.

coveragePoints.title: Stale temporary plans are reconciled or removed
coveragePoints.source_section: Immediate cleanup before the next evolution
coveragePoints.explanation: Imperative working docs with conflicting status, such as unresolved conflict records versus no-conflict records, should be reconciled and archived or deleted when served.

coveragePoints.title: Canonical docs expose stable addressable claims
coveragePoints.explanation: Canonical docs need durable claim identifiers such as DOC-REQ, CONTRACT, INV, and NON-GOAL rather than only run-local DESIGN-REQ IDs.

coveragePoints.title: Doc index emits machine-readable authority graph
coveragePoints.explanation: A doc-index capability should produce a machine-readable index over stable claims and authority relationships for downstream slicing and verification.

stories.description: As a MoonSpec maintainer, I want the documentation standard and templates to use one coherent naming and metadata model so future doc-native automation starts from unambiguous canonical guidance.

sourceReference.title: MM-927: Moon Spec Doc Architecture Alignment
sections: Immediate cleanup before the next evolution

stories.independentTest: Run a documentation-focused test or review that checks DocumentationArchitecture.md and templates for the chosen module architecture filename, system/design distinction, canonical header fields, and absence or resolved status of stale temporary conflict plans.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 3.2 and downstream examples consistently use <ModuleName>ModuleArchitecture.md as the preferred module architecture filename.
The standard distinguishes durable <SystemName>System.md documents from transitional <FeatureName>Design.md proposal documents without weakening existing viewpoint rules.
Canonical documentation templates include the metadata fields required by the standard: Authority, Owning Surface, Related Docs, and Related Implementation.
Conflicting temporary planning records about documentation-authority conflicts are reconciled, archived, or removed while preserving desired-state framing in canonical docs.
No new durable spec.md file or specs/ directory is created as part of this stabilization story.
requirements: Update canonical documentation and templates only where they define target-state documentation conventions.
Keep imperative migration or cleanup notes out of canonical docs except as stable declarative rules.
Preserve the Document Model as the lifecycle and precedence authority refined by the Documentation Architecture Standard.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Keeps canonical docs as higher authority while editing only durable target-state guidance.
Maintains document class and read-order consistency.
Owns the module architecture filename inconsistency.
Owns the system document versus feature design clarification.

assumptions: Existing documentation files and templates are the implementation surface for this story.

stories.description: As a MoonSpec workflow author, I want canonical docs to expose stable claim identifiers and a machine-readable index so story slices and verification can reference durable doc claims instead of run-local coverage IDs.

sourceReference.title: MM-927: Moon Spec Doc Architecture Alignment

stories.independentTest: Run the doc-index capability against representative canonical docs and assert that stable DOC-REQ, CONTRACT, INV, and NON-GOAL claims are discovered with source path, section, authority, and relationship metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical documentation supports a lightweight stable claim convention for requirement, contract, invariant, and non-goal identifiers.
A doc-index capability emits machine-readable JSON for discovered claims without embedding large document bodies into workflow payloads.
The index includes enough source metadata for downstream breakdown, slicing, verification, and reconciliation to cite the owning document and section.
Docs without stable claim IDs either fail with an actionable message or are reported as needing claim assignment; the tool does not silently invent canonical IDs for non-file Jira or inline text.
requirements: Define and parse stable claim ID prefixes used by canonical docs.
Represent claim identity, source path, section, summary, class, and authority relationships in a portable JSON artifact.
Keep generated index output as a temporary artifact or compact reference, not as the canonical source of truth.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the doc-index machine-readable authority artifact.

assumptions: Documentation standard stabilization should land first so the index parses the intended canonical conventions.

stories.summary: Create doc-native MoonSpec slices and implementation packets
stories.description: As a MoonSpec operator, I want MoonSpec to slice canonical documentation claims into independently testable story candidates and implementation packets so spec.md is no longer the conceptual center of the workflow.

sourceReference.title: MM-927: Moon Spec Doc Architecture Alignment

stories.independentTest: Given an indexed canonical doc set with stable claims, run the slice generation path and verify that each output story candidate references source claim IDs, carries an implementation packet, preserves coverage, and treats any generated spec.md as temporary adapter output only if still required.
acceptanceCriteria: MoonSpec models architecture descriptions, viewpoints, views, module doc sets, module contracts, claims, slices, implementation packets, and reconciliation results as native workflow concepts.
A doc slice maps one or more stable canonical claims to one independently testable story candidate with explicit dependencies and coverage.
Generated implementation packets preserve source document and claim references for downstream specify, plan, tasks, implementation, and verify stages.
spec.md, if produced for compatibility, is clearly marked and handled as a temporary derived artifact rather than the authoritative design object.
requirements: Consume doc-index output as the source of durable claim identity.
Emit doc slices and implementation packets under artifact paths, with compact references suitable for workflow payloads.
Maintain the coverage gate from canonical claims and run-local coverage points to story candidates.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the doc-native MoonSpec domain model and spec.md inversion.
Uses stable claims as slice input.
Uses the machine-readable authority graph as slice input.

assumptions: The first implementation can keep adapter compatibility with existing temporary spec artifacts while shifting authority to doc slices.

stories.summary: Align MoonSpec skills around doc-source execution
stories.description: As a MoonSpec maintainer, I want breakdown, specify, implement, verify, and reconcile to consume doc-native source references consistently so every downstream artifact can be checked against the original canonical document claims.

sourceReference.title: MM-927: Moon Spec Doc Architecture Alignment

stories.independentTest: Run a controlled MoonSpec workflow from a canonical doc slice through specify/plan/tasks/implement/verify/reconcile and assert that each artifact preserves source document, source class, stable claim IDs, run-local coverage, discovery ledger, and source-drift verdicts.
acceptanceCriteria: moonspec-breakdown keeps imperative-input fail-fast behavior and emits temporary breakdown artifacts with source document class and coverage ownership.
moonspec-specify records canonical source document and claim references while treating spec.md as temporary execution material.
moonspec-implement writes a discovery ledger for doc drift using source references rather than uncited observations.
moonspec-verify reports Source Document Drift against the preserved original source and claim coverage.
moonspec-doc-reconcile updates canonical docs only when verified evidence definitely requires it.
requirements: Preserve sourceReference.path and claim IDs for file-backed docs; preserve Jira title/key with null path and empty claim IDs for trusted Jira text.
Keep large source content out of workflow history by passing artifact references or compact metadata at workflow boundaries.
Add or update workflow/activity or adapter-boundary tests for changed MoonSpec skill contracts.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns breakdown classification, fail-fast behavior, and temporary breakdown output.
Owns source traceability through specify, implement, verify, and reconcile.
Applies doc-native source references across the skill chain.

assumptions: Existing MoonSpec skill names remain canonical and are updated directly rather than through compatibility aliases.

stories.summary: Enforce doc reconciliation evidence before PR completion
stories.description: As a MoonMind operator, I want orchestrated Jira MoonSpec runs to reconcile canonical docs and commit required doc edits before PR creation so completed implementation work closes with docs and code aligned.

sourceReference.title: MM-927: Moon Spec Doc Architecture Alignment

stories.independentTest: Run or unit-test the Jira MoonSpec orchestration preset with a completed implementation result and verify that doc reconciliation runs before PR creation, writes a structured reconciliation artifact, and includes required doc edits in the same commit boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: moonspec-orchestrate includes doc reconciliation as a final stage after implementation verification succeeds.
The Jira orchestrate preset runs reconciliation before PR creation and records a structured reconciliation artifact.
Required reconciliation doc edits are committed with the implementation changes in the same PR boundary.
The workflow fails or reports a clear blocked state when reconciliation evidence is missing, ambiguous, or conflicts with higher-authority docs.
Operator-visible output confirms that /speckit.verify compared final behavior against the original preserved design before reconciliation is trusted.
requirements: Preserve reconciliation as an evidence-backed stage rather than an implementation diary in canonical docs.
Keep PR publishing and commit behavior idempotent and policy-gated.
Expose reconciliation artifacts through durable artifact references.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Keeps canonical docs authoritative at PR completion.
Uses verify and reconcile evidence from the skill chain.
Owns orchestration and Jira preset reconciliation-before-PR behavior.
Completes the doc-native loop from docs to reconciliation result.

assumptions: PR creation itself is a later workflow step and is outside this breakdown story execution.

outOfScope: No spec.md files are created during this breakdown.
No specs/ directories are created during this breakdown.
No Jira issues, implementation plans, tasks, code changes, PRs, or Jira transitions are produced by this breakdown step.
Provider-native Jira or Atlassian connectors are not used; the trusted MoonMind previous-step Jira context is the selected source.
